perturbation.original_text: 4.1 Party A and Party B unanimously agree that they will allocate the proceeds generated from cooperation in accordance with thefollowing provisions: Party B shall have the right to charge, on a quarterly basis, the service fee ("Service Fee") from Party A or its subsidiaries who have accepted Party B's Services, or designate another person to charge Service Fee from Party A or its subsidiaries who have accepted Party B's Services. The total sum of Service Fee shall be equal to the amount of profits gained by Party A or its subsidiaries who have accepted Party B's Services. Party B shall have the right to adjust the amount of Service Fee at its own discretion, without the prior consent of Party A or its subsidiaries.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.1 Party A and Party B unanimously agree that they will allocate the proceeds generated from cooperation in accordance with the following provisions: Party B shall have the right to charge, on a quarterly basis, the service fee ("Service Fee") from Party A or its subsidiaries who have accepted Party B's Services, or designate another person to charge Service Fee from Party A or its subsidiaries who have accepted Party B's Services. The total sum of Service Fee shall be equal to the amount of profits gained by Party A or its subsidiaries who have accepted Party B's Services. Party B shall only adjust the amount of Service Fee with the prior consent of Party A or its subsidiaries.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that Party B has the right to adjust the Service Fee at its own discretion, without the prior consent of Party A or its subsidiaries. The modified version states that Party B can only adjust the Service Fee with the prior consent of Party A or its subsidiaries. This creates an in-text contradiction regarding Party B's ability to adjust the Service Fee, specifically where it says 'Party B shall have the right to adjust the amount of Service Fee at its own discretion, without the prior consent of Party A or its subsidiaries.' which is a direct contradiction to what was stated.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.1 Party A and Party B unanimously agree that they will allocate the proceeds generated from cooperation in accordance with the following provisions: Party B shall have the right to charge, on a quarterly basis, the service fee ("Service Fee") from Party A or its subsidiaries who have accepted Party B's Services, or designate another person to charge Service Fee from Party A or its subsidiaries who have accepted Party B's Services. The total sum of Service Fee shall be equal to the amount of profits gained by Party A or its subsidiaries who have accepted Party B's Services. Party B shall only adjust the amount of Service Fee with the prior consent of Party A or its subsidiaries.
perturbation.justification: The modified text changes Party B's ability to adjust the service fee from 'at its own discretion, without prior consent' to 'only with the prior consent'. The contradicted text, which is identical to the changed text, also states Party B can 'only adjust the amount of Service Fee with the prior consent of Party A or its subsidiaries.' Because the original text gave Party B discretion and the new text and contradicted text limit this discretion by requiring consent, there is a strong in-text contradiction according to the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction, where 'a clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications'. Even though the changed and contradicted text are identical, the original text provides a contradictory intention.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Term of Cooperation Party A and Party B agree and confirm that the term of cooperation under this Agreement shall commence from the execution date hereof and end on the expiration date of the operation term of Party B ("Term of Cooperation").
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Term of Cooperation Party A and Party B agree and confirm that the term of cooperation under this Agreement shall commence from the execution date hereof and end five years from the execution date ("Term of Cooperation").
perturbation.explanation: The original text defines the Term of Cooperation as ending on the expiration date of the operation term of Party B. The modified text defines it as ending five years from the execution date. This creates a contradiction on how long the agreement will remain in place. The specific contradiction occurs where the term is defined differently which creates two different end dates.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Term of Cooperation Party A and Party B agree and confirm that the term of cooperation under this Agreement shall commence from the execution date hereof and end five years from the execution date ("Term of Cooperation").
perturbation.justification: The changed text defines the term of cooperation as ending five years from the execution date. The contradicted text states the same thing which would not normally cause a contradiction. However, the original text which stated the term of cooperation would end on the expiration date of Party B, was modified to create the contradictory 'five years from execution date' statement. Because the modified text and the contradicted text are the same, it is still a contradiction, caused by the change. The term is now defined in two separate ways, potentially leading to disputes about the contract's duration. This aligns with the structural flaws definition which states that clauses placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications creates uncertainty about the obligations of each party.

perturbation.original_text: 6.1 Prior to the expiration of the Term of Cooperation , this Agreement shall only be terminated upon occurrence of the followingcircumstances: 6.1.1 Party B shall have the right to terminate this Agreement in advance without the prior written consent from Party A, bysending a written notice to Party A but Party A may not terminate or rescind this Agreement;
perturbation.changed_text: 6.1 Prior to the expiration of the Term of Cooperation, this Agreement may be terminated by either party for any reason with 30 days written notice. 6.1.1 Party B shall have the right to terminate this Agreement in advance without the prior written consent from Party A, by sending a written notice to Party A but Party A may not terminate or rescind this Agreement;
perturbation.explanation: The original text stated that the agreement can only be terminated under specific circumstances (listed in 6.1.1, 6.1.2, 6.1.3). The modified text introduces a new condition: either party can terminate for *any* reason with 30 days' notice. This directly contradicts the original limitations on termination. The in-text contradiction is in the clause saying one party can terminate for any reason, making the original clauses redundant.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.1 Prior to the expiration of the Term of Cooperation, this Agreement may be terminated by either party for any reason with 30 days written notice. 6.1.1 Party B shall have the right to terminate this Agreement in advance without the prior written consent from Party A, by sending a written notice to Party A but Party A may not terminate or rescind this Agreement;
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a clause stating that either party can terminate the agreement for any reason with 30 days notice. This directly contradicts the original text (mirrored in the contradicted text), which stipulates that the agreement can only be terminated under specific circumstances outlined in subsequent sub-clauses (6.1.1, etc.). The new clause renders these specific circumstances redundant and creates a direct conflict within the agreement, creating a structural flaw and resulting in an in-text contradiction.
